query.sentence:
Beyond PEEK, which thermoplastic materials can:
- Withstand continuous or intermittent use up to 200 °C
- Offer good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils
- Be co extruded with softer polymers such as TPU, TPE, or TPV (e.g., in multilayer or jacketed constructions)
query.application_Y: Withstand 200°C, good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils, co extrusion with softer polymers

properties: chemical resistance to acids, bases, organic solvents, and oils
high dimensional stability and mechanical strength at elevated temperatures
melt flow index compatible with multilayer extrusion (≈10–30 g/10 min at 230 °C)
good adhesion to softer polymers (TPU, TPE, TPV) in co‑extrusion
constraints: must not degrade below 200 °C during service
must be available in grades suitable for lab‑scale extrusion
cost must remain within the same order of magnitude as PEEK (≈$10–20/kg)

final_candidate.justification: Ultem 5000 has a glass‑transition temperature of 217 °C and can be used continuously at 200 °C. It offers excellent resistance to acids, bases, organic solvents, and oils, with negligible moisture uptake (<0.05 %). Its melt flow index (~12 g/10 min at 230 °C) is suitable for multilayer extrusion. Commercially available compatibilizers (PEI/TPU block copolymers or maleic anhydride grafted PEI) enable strong interlayer adhesion with TPU, TPE, and TPV.

process_recipe.description: Dry Ultem 5000 pellets (≤5 % moisture) in a vacuum oven at 80 °C for 2 h.
Dry TPU (or TPE/TPV) pellets in a vacuum oven at 70 °C for 1 h.
Blend a compatibilizer (e.g., PEI/TPU block copolymer) at 1–2 wt% with the TPU stream to improve adhesion.
Co‑extrude using a twin‑screw extruder (10–12 mm barrel). Set temperature profile: 350–360 °C for the PEI zone, 190–210 °C for the TPU zone. Maintain screw speed 200–250 rpm.
Calibrate die to achieve desired layer thickness (e.g., 70 µm PEI / 30 µm TPU).
Cool the extruded multilayer film in a water bath at 20–25 °C, then wind onto spools.
